journal_entry.reason: Atlas received $5,000 cash deposit for moving services to be performed in one month. Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. Since the services have not yet been performed, the deposit creates a liability (Unearned Revenue). The bank transfer increases the Chequing Account asset. No tax is mentioned in the transaction, so no tax lines are added.

resolved_ambiguities.resolution: Deposit received before service delivery is conventionally treated as unearned revenue (liability) under the revenue recognition principle — revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied, not when cash is received
Bank transfer to chequing account clearly indicates the cash account to be debited

output.asset_increase_reason: Cash/Bank (chequing account) increases by $5,000 deposit received from customer
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals in this transaction
output.expense_increase_reason: No expenses incurred; this is a cash receipt transaction, not an expense
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid down or reduced; instead a liability (unearned revenue) is being created on the credit side
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are decreased in this transaction
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue is being decreased; the services have not yet been performed so no revenue is recognized. The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue) until services are performed

output.liability_increase_reason: The $5,000 deposit received creates a liability (unearned revenue/deferred revenue/customer deposit) because Atlas has an obligation to provide moving services in the future. The service has not yet been performed, so this is not revenue yet under IFRS.
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity increase. This is a customer deposit for services, not an owner investment or other equity transaction.
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is recognized yet. Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when (or as) performance obligations are satisfied. The moving services will be provided in one month, so the performance obligation has not been satisfied at the time of deposit receipt.
output.asset_decrease_reason: No asset decrease. Cash (chequing account) is increasing on the debit side, not decreasing.
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividend/drawing transaction involved. This is a customer deposit for services.
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expense decrease. This is receipt of a customer deposit, not a refund or reversal of an expense.

output.incomplete_information_reasoning: The transaction is clear. Atlas received $5,000 cash as a deposit for moving services to be performed in one month. Under IFRS 15, this is unearned revenue (a liability) because the performance obligation has not yet been satisfied. No additional business facts are needed to determine the entry structure.

debit_asset_increase.reason: Accept. One asset increases: Cash (or Chequing Account) receives the $5,000 deposit via bank transfer.

debit_dividend_increase.reason: Accept. No dividend distribution in this transaction.

debit_expense_increase.reason: Accept. No expense incurred in this transaction.

debit_liability_decrease.reason: Accept. No liability is being reduced in this transaction.

debit_equity_decrease.reason: Accept. No equity reduction in this transaction.

debit_revenue_decrease.reason: Accept. No revenue reversal in this transaction.

credit_liability_increase.reason: Accept. One liability increases: Unearned Revenue (or Deferred Revenue, Customer Deposits) for the $5,000 deposit received before services are performed. Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied, which will occur in one month.

credit_equity_increase.reason: Accept. No equity increase in this transaction.

credit_revenue_increase.reason: Accept. No revenue is recognized at this point. The performance obligation (moving services) has not been satisfied. Revenue will be recognized when the services are performed in one month.

credit_asset_decrease.reason: Accept. No asset is being reduced in this transaction.

credit_dividend_decrease.reason: Accept. No dividend reduction in this transaction.

credit_expense_decrease.reason: Accept. No expense reversal in this transaction.

entry.reason: Atlas received $5,000 cash deposit for moving services to be performed in one month. Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. Since the services have not yet been performed, the deposit creates a liability (Unearned Revenue). The bank transfer increases the Chequing Account asset. No tax is mentioned in the transaction, so no tax lines are added.

output.decision_rationale: The transaction is straightforward and complete. Atlas received a $5,000 deposit for services to be performed in the future. Under IFRS 15 revenue recognition principles, this creates a liability (Unearned Revenue) until the performance obligation is satisfied. The classifier tuples correctly identified one asset increase (cash) and one liability increase (unearned revenue). Total debits ($5,000) equal total credits ($5,000). No tax is mentioned, so no tax lines are required. All necessary business facts are present in the transaction text.
